name: Capybara
description: Acceptance-test framework for Rack-based web applications that drives headless browsers via Selenium, Cuprite or Apparition with an intuitive Ruby DSL.

seo.title: Capybara — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: Capybara: Acceptance-test framework for Rack-based web applications that drives headless browsers via Selenium, Cuprite or Apparition with an intuitive Ruby DSL

when_to_use: End-to-end acceptance tests for Rails applications
Drive headless Chrome with an intuitive Ruby DSL
Simulate user journeys in feature and integration specs
Swap browser drivers without changing test code

pros: Expressive Ruby DSL for user-centric acceptance tests
Deep Rails and RSpec integration out of the box
Great implicit waiting eliminates most sleep() hacks
cons: Flaky when tests leak JavaScript or DB state
Driver-specific bugs force you to know internals
Slower than pure unit tests, be choosy what you cover
review.verdict: Still the gold standard for Rails end-to-end tests, if you accept the flakiness tax of real browsers.
review.best_for: Rails teams writing feature specs that mirror actual user journeys
review.not_for: Non-Ruby stacks or teams preferring Playwright/Cypress for E2E coverage
